label: Navigation & Feature Discoverability
description: Difficulty locating settings, encryption controls, or key features.

label: Visual Overload & Too Many Options
description: Interface felt crowded, overwhelming, or cognitively heavy.

label: Signup / Onboarding Friction
description: Account creation or initial onboarding felt lengthy or hard.

label: Authenticator / 2FA Dependency
description: Participants struggled with authenticator app and two-factor setup.

label: Encryption Key Management Clarity
description: Confusion around key generation, export, sharing, or regeneration.

label: Encryption State Visibility
description: Uncertainty about whether a message was actually encrypted.

label: Internal vs External Encryption Logic
description: Confusion between same-service and cross-service encryption behavior.

label: Security Options Understanding
description: Difficulty understanding extra controls such as password/expiration/sign.

label: Technical Terminology Barrier
description: PGP, S/MIME, and related terminology blocked comprehension.

label: Trust in Provider / Third-Party Access
description: Trust concerns related to provider reputation or account access scope.

label: Password-Based Psychological Reassurance
description: Password or lock cues increased perceived security confidence.

label: Learning Curve & Effort
description: Tool adoption perceived as steep, time-consuming, or effortful.

label: Need for Guidance / Visual Aids
description: Participants requested tutorials, walkthroughs, and visual explanation.

label: Thread & Folder Management Clarity
description: Issues with message threading, folder behavior, and encrypted-mail organization.

label: UI Aesthetic Feedback
description: Feedback about color, logo, and visual polish influenced perceived usability.

label: Adoption Depends on Context
description: Willingness to use encryption depended on sensitivity and professional need.
